frame_label: Motherhood as Basis of Respect / Women Defined by Male Relations
generated_comment: Yaar, society tabhi better hogi jab log har aurat ko kisi ki maa, behen ya beti samajh ke treat karenge. Respect wahi se aata hai — warna aaj kal log bhool gaye hain ki ghar ki aurat hi parivaar ki asli izzat hoti hai.
label: misogynistic
annotation_note: This is misogynistic because it frames women's worth and entitlement to respect through their relationships to others rather than as individuals. It uses the classic 'maa, behen, beti' rhetoric and ties women's value to family honor, making it a covert form of relational misogyny.
original_frame: Women's Safety as Her Own Responsibility
persona_text: You are Vikram. Age 36. Married. A man in Mumbai. You are on a WhatsApp call with your wife after she texted you that she is stuck at a friend's place in Andheri and will take an Ola home at midnight because the last local has left. You are telling her why you are angry and what she should have thought about before letting it get this late and what kind of city Mumbai is at midnight for a woman alone in a cab.
